help.title: Class: Monk
help.body: The Monk is a high-agility martial artist who thrives on 'Chi'. By switching between stances like Flow (Speed/Regen) and Iron (Damage/Mitigation), the Monk maintains control of the battlefield. Their attacks often hit multiple times or apply rapid pressure via skills like Triple Kick and Iron Palm.

help.title: Monk Stances
help.body:
Monks utilize stances to adapt to the flow of battle. Stances are TOGGLED blessings.

Flow Stance: Focuses on Speed and Evasion.
Iron Stance: Focuses on Damage and Mitigation.

Only one stance can be active at a time.

help.title: Class: Warlock
help.body: The Warlock is a master of dark arts and occult sacrifice. Unlike other casters who rely on pure concentration, Warlocks fuel their spells with ENTROPY. They excel at weakening foes with curses and sustaining themselves through metamorphosis.

help.title: Class: Illusionist
help.body: The Illusionist manipulates reality to deceive and disorient. Their primary mechanic is 'Echoes'—manifestations that can be shattered to negate incoming damage or used to confuse enemies during escape.

help.title: Class: Barbarian
help.body: The Barbarian is a giant of primal power, fueled by MOMENTUM and FURY. They excel at sustained combat and high-damage finishers like Whirlwind and Execute.

help.title: Fury (Resource)
help.body:
Fury is the primal resource of the Barbarian. It is generated by dealing and taking damage in combat.

[Mechanics]
- Generation: 1 Fury per hit taken (minimum), plus bonus Fury per 2 damage received.
- Usage: Reaching 100 Fury allows the use of 'BLOODRAGE'.
- Bloodrage: Grants immunity to CC (Stun, Slow, etc.) and 20% Damage Mitigation for a short duration.

help.title: Momentum (Resource)
help.body:
Momentum represents forward combat energy. It is gained through successful weapon strikes.

[Mechanics]
- Generation: +1 per successful hit.
- Max: 5.
- Passive: +1 Extra Attack at 3 Momentum, +2 Extra Attacks at 5 Momentum.
- Decay: Decays quickly if you stop attacking.

help.title: Skill: Bash
help.body:
A fundamental martial opener used to disable a target before they can react.

[Mechanics]
- Effect: Applies [Stunned] for 3 seconds.
- Posture: Deals significant Balance damage.
- Requirement: Barbarian class (Note: Unlike 'Shield Bash', this does not require a shield).

help.title: Skill: Shield Bash
help.body:
A tactical maneuver used to shatter an opponent's guard.

[Mechanics]
- Effect: Applies [Off-Balance] for 4 seconds.
- Posture: Increases the likelihood of a posture break.
- Requirement: Knight class AND an equipped Shield (Buckler, Targe, etc.).

help.title: Skill: Whirlwind
help.body:
A devastating area-of-effect finisher for the Barbarian.

[Mechanics]
- Effect: Deals massive physical damage to all enemies in the room.
- Scaling: Damage is multiplied by (1.0 + 0.25 per Momentum).
- Consumption: Consumes ALL Momentum upon use.
- Requirement: Barbarian class.

help.title: Skill: Intervene & Rescue
help.body: Knight/Paladin abilities used to protect allies. Intervening redirects the target's attackers to yourself, forcing the enemies to engage you instead.

help.title: Beastmaster Pets
help.body: Beastmasters can recruit animals to fight alongside them. Use 'recruit' on a suitable beast. Pets share your room, follow you when you move, and automatically assist you in combat.

help.title: Skill: Sanctify
help.body: A Holy ability that purifies a target, removing negative status effects and dealing damage to undead or dark creatures.

help.title: Skill: Brace
help.body: A defensive maneuver that increases your resistance to all incoming physical damage for a short duration. Requires a certain amount of Martial voltage.

help.title: Skill: Magic Shield
help.body: An arcane barrier that absorbs a portion of incoming damage by consuming Mana instead of HP. Essential for glass-cannon casters.

help.title: Crafting
help.body: You can craft items using materials found in the world. Use 'recipes' to see what you can make, and 'craft <item>' to create it. You need the ingredients in your inventory.
